subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Data Detectives organize class survey results for the school fair.
lesson.mini_lesson: Graphs and tables organize counts so you can compare categories and answer questions.
lesson.objective: Represent and interpret data using picture graphs, bar graphs, line plots, and data tables.
lesson.concept_explanation: Picture graphs use symbols, bar graphs use bars, line plots use X marks, and data tables list counts. Use labels, keys, and scales before answering.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may count one symbol as one even when the graph key uses a different scale, or compare the wrong categories.
lesson.real_world_connection: Students use graphs to summarize favorite snacks, books, sports, and measurements.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the labels, use the key or scale, find the category count, and subtract to compare how many more or less.
hints: Read the title and labels first.
Use the key or scale before counting.
For more or less, subtract the smaller count from the larger count.

worked_examples.text: If a picture graph key says ⭐ = 2 votes, then 4 stars show 8 votes.
If apples have 9 votes and grapes have 5 votes, apples have 4 more votes because 9 - 5 = 4.
On a line plot, each X over a number means one measurement with that value.

hint_ladder: Read the title and labels first.
Use the key or scale before counting.
For more or less, subtract the smaller count from the larger count.

misconception_bank.graph_scale_confusion: Learner may ignore the graph key or bar scale.
misconception_bank.category_count_error: Learner may count the wrong category.
misconception_bank.more_less_data_confusion: Learner may add instead of subtract for more/less questions.
misconception_bank.line_plot_mark_error: Learner may place or count X marks at the wrong number.
